title: Drip vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Constant Contact: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your business needs best.
h1: Drip vs Constant Contact: A Detailed Comparison
intro: Drip and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they cater to different business needs. Drip specializes in e-commerce automation with advanced revenue tracking, while Constant Contact offers broader digital marketing tools for nonprofits and local businesses. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing goals and budget.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce businesses with deep store integration needs

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce business and need advanced revenue tracking and store integration
You want sophisticated automation workflows with visual builders for complex campaigns
You prioritize platform ratings and customer satisfaction scores
choose_b_if: You have a tight budget and are looking for the most affordable entry point
You're a nonprofit or local business needing multiple marketing channels beyond email
You prefer an established platform with a longer track record (founded 1995)

faq.q: Is Drip better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your business type. Drip is superior for e-commerce businesses requiring advanced tracking and automation, with higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.4 vs 4.0 on G2). However, Constant Contact offers broader digital marketing features and is more budget-friendly for small nonprofits and local businesses.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact is significantly cheaper, starting at $12/month compared to Drip's $39/month. If cost is your primary concern and you need basic email marketing, Constant Contact is the more affordable option. However, Drip's higher price reflects its specialized e-commerce features and stronger ratings.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your email lists and contacts from Constant Contact to Drip. Most email platforms support standard import formats like CSV files. However, Constant Contact's campaign history and detailed analytics won't transfer, so you'll start fresh with automation workflows in Drip. Check both platforms' migration guides for specific instructions.
